item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm amazed that the author of this managed to make the most boring and overrated book I have ever read into something interesting without taking the sleeper cell activation path.

I was especially glad that the name in the book wasn't Mark, John, Robert, or Peter

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

The collaboration Visions of a Better World contains one entry about a massive containment breach of SCP-1082. Linking from this skip to there not only would add to the potential story of this article, but also given the sheer number of links contained in VoaBW, would link into a major wikiwalk hub. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Individuals not treated with amnesiac agents within forty-eight hours begin to display secondary symptoms. These symptoms take the form of an attack of delusional paranoia, convincing affected individuals that a mysterious worldwide conspiracy exists to suppress knowledge of SCP-1082 and preserve what they believe to be a "status quo" based on deception, to the detriment of the human race. Due to current Foundation efforts to contain SCP-1082, affected individuals begin to demonstrate particular resentment toward the Foundation and Foundation personnel. The culmination of this resentment in all cases leads to violent attempts to breach containment for SCP-1082 and expose as many individuals as possible to its effects.

To this:

Individuals not treated with amnesiac agents within forty-eight hours begin to display secondary symptoms. These symptoms take the form of an attack of delusional paranoia, convincing affected individuals that a mysterious worldwide conspiracy exists to suppress knowledge of SCP-1082 and preserve what they believe to be a "status quo" based on deception, to the detriment of the human race. Due to current Foundation efforts to contain SCP-1082, affected individuals begin to demonstrate particular resentment toward the Foundation and Foundation personnel. The culmination of this resentment in all cases leads to violent attempts to breach containment for SCP-1082 and expose as many individuals as possible to its effects.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: I actually like this
item.content:encoded: The great thing about this is that you're not sure it isn't like SCP-2047 or 8900, where what we consider normal and natural is actually the anomaly. You're left seriously considering the idea that those "infected" are right, that they're the way the world ought to be, and deception is the real anomaly, that breached containment before we realized what had happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article actually predates that one by a year and a half. Although I swear there's another -J that's a book (an Ayn Rand novel?) that has similar effects to what we see in the document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically it's an autism-maker

Not even subtle about it.

The early stages basically sounds like normal people with varying levels of autism. Then the document just reads like elaboration squirrel without the humor and with added paranoia.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, so I read all of this fully prepared for some sort of parody of something that generally makes people crazy anyway. I finished reading, clicked one of the several links, and was brought to something about a book called "The Catcher in the Rye". I have not read this book, so any joke or parody sailed so far over my head I could see it out of my peripheral vision. I could only say, "Wait, what?" I assumed that I would get the joke if I knew what the book was about, so I subsequently looked up a plot summary, but I still don't understand what this book has to do with honesty. All the same, I'm going to assume that there is a joke or parody in there somewhere, but I need to read the whole book in order to get it.

Even if this is a joke or parody, mind you, I am not about to say it should be a -J, because the anomalous effect is anomalous enough that this would be something that actually needs containing (and anyone who says I made my last "This article should be a -J" statement just because I think the article in question is "funny" or "a parody" needs to understand that I comprehend things a lot better than that >.>). I'm upvoting both documents, as they are, and wouldn't have them changed or moved in the slightest. Okay? :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not a fan of links that consist of the word here. It is a personal pet peeve of mine. It just says late 90s geocities to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for Document 1082-25. Without it, I wouldn't have voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is infectious information, so you are wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As per Understanding Memetics, this SCP does NOT exhibit a memetic effect. Original author, please correct.

I stand corrected. Thanks, Sorts!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this one. It's not the most original concept in the world, but the details and execution are very well done. And yeah, the document helps - I read that first, in fact, so I approve of the link at the top of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey Eskobar! You'll probably get more upvotes if you put the document on this page… just saying…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am very conflicted about this. The main article is very similar to 370, and attached document - to 931 (and, by proxy, to the Toaster). Also, the main article don't work at all without the document, and that is wrong. I think I would have liked a tale based on this concept. The way it is now I really don't know if I like it or not.

Downvoting the article and upvoting the document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And this article got me my fourth karma pip thing. I should post two pages simultaneously more often!

/joke

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't read the book, but I really like this. Kudos on the well written report.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Looks like someone hasn't read Catcher in the Rye…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one… I'm not 100% sure why, but I do. +1

Having clicked the incredibly verbose test file first in the "recently created" page, I was waiting for it to be some sort of meta-piece explaining a rash of wordy, unwieldly new entries lately…. Which (done well) would also have gotten a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's a book that turns you into Holden Caufield? (or, rather, what Holden imagines himself to be?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice-well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like it. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would imagine they would assign personnel to the project they are okay with risking. Besides, only the few people who actually guard/administer the item need to know anything beyond the basic document. With testing indefinitely suspended, I'm not even sure there's anyone required to know what's in it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changes made. I had imagined the number being about 12% or so, but that wasn't clear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better. I'd put it below the rating module, though. Also, it seems odd that the Foundation is requiring personnel to read a document that has a double-digit chance of leading to nasty infection and administration of high-powered amnesiacs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought about including it as a collapsible, but thought it would make it a big bulky. I think I can make it more prominent, though. One minute…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you read the attached document? I hate how integral it has to be to understanding the article, but I tried to make it/them as accessible as possible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice article. I like the attached document, though it's pretty critical to the main one. Perhaps enough that you might want to trim down the main article and make the link more prominent to better emphasize its importance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Downvoted due to similarities to SCP-055…whatever it is.
